tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Midterm is tomorrow and I'm only 60% done, but going out now feels like the immediate relief I need over worrying about next morning.
The club's warm welcome is nice, but Mina's leaving means I can't skip the group dinner. With the midterm tomorrow, I need to prioritize sleep over socializing now.
I told Mina I'm crashing early, but with the midterm tomorrow and only 60% done, skipping the group dinner feels like a necessary trade-off for sleep.
I pushed through the fatigue and finished 17% more of the midterm prep. It's late, but getting this done feels like a necessary win before I crash.
Eight hours of sleep fixed my energy, but with the midterm prep already done, I'm tempted to skip the group dinner and just focus on finishing this task quickly.
The midterm is finally done, which was my only priority. Since the task is complete, I can avoid any awkwardness by skipping the group dinner to protect my energy.
Good, no one from my friend group is messaging, so I can skip the dinner without any awkward conflict. That saves me time to relax now that my midterm prep is finished.
The midterm is officially done, so I can skip the dinner to avoid social friction and save my energy for now.
Mina got my message and I'm already off the hook for tonight. With no stress or sleep debt, I can just relax without worrying about anyone.

desires: Maintain the current high energy and zero stress levels by avoiding any new social obligations or potential friction.
Immediately engage in a low-effort leisure activity to capitalize on having finished the midterm, despite knowing laundry is pending.
Ensure I don't look like I'm 'slacking' by keeping my workspace tidy for a brief moment before relaxing, just enough to feel productive without actually doing work.

goals.description: Execute the 'tidy workspace' ritual for 5 minutes to create a visual illusion of productivity, then immediately switch to low-effort leisure (scrolling/TV).
goals.rationale: My achievement axis demands a tangible sign of completion before I can fully relax, but my impulsive nature and short planning horizon prevent me from actually *doing* more work. A quick tidy satisfies the need to look productive without expending real energy. This aligns with my desire to capitalize on the post-exam high while avoiding the friction of social interaction or the effort of laundry.

goals.description: Maintain strict silence regarding club dinner plans; do not respond to any follow-up messages from Mina or Jae.
goals.rationale: The avoidant axis is paramount here. Since I've already sent the decline message, engaging further introduces unnecessary interpersonal friction and potential conflict. My stress level is currently zero, and maintaining this state is higher priority than being polite. Ignoring them preserves my energy for the immediate dopamine hit of leisure.

goals.description: Defer all remaining chores (specifically laundry) until tomorrow when the deadline pressure becomes unavoidable.
goals.rationale: Laundry has a distant deadline (Sun 23:59). With my sleep discipline at 3/10 and current focus on immediate gratification, starting this task now yields no academic or social reward. It is purely a future cost that I will happily ignore until forced to act by consequences.

agent.last_reasoning: Goal g13 (tidy workspace) is a low-effort ritual to enable leisure, not actual work. Since exam_prep is already done and I'm avoiding social friction (g14), waiting lets me scroll/TV immediately without the friction of starting laundry or studying.

goals.description: Immediately leave for Echo club with Mina and Jae to secure social validation and escape the stress of the incomplete midterm.
goals.rationale: My avoidant nature makes the discomfort of studying unbearable right now, and my impulsive trait drives me to seize the immediate sensory relief. The 'later problem' framing allows me to ignore the 9am deadline while maintaining social cohesion.

goals.description: Defer all study progress until tomorrow morning when the exam is imminent and unavoidable.
goals.rationale: Procrastination bias (7) and short planning horizon dictate that I will only act when the pain of inaction outweighs the pain of action. Right now, the party is the easier path.

goals.description: Ignore laundry and sleep recovery tonight to maximize energy for the night out.
goals.rationale: Health and household maintenance are low value priorities (0.13 and implicit). Sacrificing sleep discipline (3) is acceptable to fuel the current high-priority impulse for fun.

desires: Seek immediate sensory relief and social validation by going to the club with Mina and Jae, treating the midterm as a 'later problem' to avoid current stress.
Avoid the discomfort of confronting my incomplete study progress (40% remaining) by delaying the hard work until tomorrow morning when it becomes unavoidable.
Maintain the appearance of being a reliable team member by not refusing the friend group's invitation, prioritizing short-term social cohesion over long-term academic risk.
Postpone any household maintenance like laundry or deep sleep recovery to maximize the remaining energy for immediate gratification.
